summary: The reproduction shows that a read-only link-share token can list sibling share hashes, exchange a disclosed admin share hash for a stronger JWT, and then use that escalated token to update the victim project.
expected_security_impact: A read-only link-share holder should be forbidden from updating the project directly, but after disclosing a sibling admin share hash through the share-listing route, the same attacker should be able to mint the admin share token and successfully update the project title.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh cloned Vikunja at commit 3ba5192b1bd0607998bdaf9259d795fb14530384, built the helper image, and executed the proof test. The run emitted the expected warning for the direct read-only update attempt, then the test passed and verify.sh printed LINK_SHARE_HASH_ESCALATION_CONFIRMED followed by the run-level SUCCESS marker.
verification_method: The helper test creates a read-only share and an admin share on the same victim project, exercises the share-listing and share-auth routes, and then distinguishes the real exploit from false positives by checking that the read-only token is denied while the escalated token succeeds on the project update path.
evidence_produced_by_run: go test -mod=mod -run TestReadOnlyShareCanEscalateViaSiblingHashDisclosure exited successfully inside the reproduction container.
The test output includes the expected warning for the direct read-only update attempt and then completes with PASS.
verify.sh printed LINK_SHARE_HASH_ESCALATION_CONFIRMED and run.sh printed the final SUCCESS marker.

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
